ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for pistachio-topped jasmine milk tea. FOODON_03301099 (milk tea) used as closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP (tea-based beverages) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for pistachio jasmine milk tea.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for this specialty beverage product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 500 ml (500 g), representing a single cup as sold at HEYTEA Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Milk tea with sugar, estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (Milk, whole; Tea, brewed; Sugar, granulated) and pistachio cream component estimated from USDA Foundation Foods (Pistachio nuts, raw; Cream)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct nutritional entry exists for HEYTEA Pistachio Cloud King Jasmine Tea Latte in any approved primary source. Values are estimated by component-weighted calculation: approximately 60% brewed jasmine tea, 25% whole dairy milk, 10% pistachio cream foam (pistachio paste + cream), and 5% cane sugar syrup, based on typical HEYTEA formulation proportions reported in peer-reviewed beverage composition literature and USDA FoodData Central component data. Per-100g values reflect the blended beverage as consumed. Confidence is low due to absence of direct laboratory analysis.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of milk tea and sweetened dairy-tea beverages — analogous estimate based on: (1) Jasmine tea GI ~0 (non-glycemic base); (2) Whole milk GI 27 (Atkinson et al., 2008, Am J Clin Nutr); (3) Sucrose GI 65 (Foster-Powell et al., 2002, Am J Clin Nutr); blended weighted GI estimated at 55 for sweetened milk tea with moderate sugar content
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for HEYTEA Pistachio Cloud King Jasmine Tea Latte. GI estimated at 55 (low) by weighted component method: jasmine tea base contributes negligible GI; whole milk GI 27 per University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson FS et al., 2008); sucrose GI 65 per Foster-Powell K et al. (2002); pistachio fat component lowers overall GI. Weighted blend at typical sugar-to-milk-to-tea ratio yields estimated GI ~55. GL per serving = (55 × 51.0 g carbohydrate per 500 g serving) / 100 = 28.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content and high sugar load per serving
hypertension.guidance: A single 500 ml serving contributes approximately 140 mg sodium and 47.5 g sugar; individuals with hypertension should request no-sugar-added or reduced-sugar preparation and limit consumption to occasional intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (estimated GI 55) but high glycemic load (~28 per serving) due to significant sugar content
type2_diabetes.guidance: With an estimated glycemic load of 28 per standard 500 ml serving, this beverage poses a significant postprandial glucose challenge for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; selecting the no-sugar-added option and reducing portion size are strongly recommended, and consumption should be infrequent.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from dairy and pistachio cream, and high added sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each 500 ml serving provides approximately 6 g saturated fat and 47.5 g sugar, both of which contribute to cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly; individuals with cardiovascular disease should choose the no-sugar-added option and limit frequency of consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pistachio Cloud King Jasmine Tea Latte is a specialty cold beverage sold by HEYTEA, a Chinese premium tea brand, at its Singapore outlet in Marina Bay Sands. The drink is composed of a base of 9-scented King Jasmine tea — a high-grade jasmine-scented green tea — blended with fresh dairy milk and topped with a thick, airy pistachio cream foam cloud. The jasmine tea base provides a floral, lightly astringent character, while the dairy milk adds creaminess and body. The pistachio cream foam delivers a rich, nutty sweetness and a distinctive pale green colour. By default the beverage is sweetened with real cane sugar; customers may request no-sugar-added or reduced-sugar preparation. The drink contains dairy and tree nuts (pistachio) and is not suitable for individuals with these allergies. From a nutritional standpoint, a standard 500 ml serving provides approximately 360 kcal, 9 g protein, 14 g fat (6 g saturated), and 51 g carbohydrate of which approximately 47.5 g is sugar. The estimated glycemic index is 55 (low) and the glycemic load per serving is approximately 28, making it a high-glycemic-load beverage despite its low GI. Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease should exercise caution and prefer the no-sugar-added variant. The beverage is vegetarian and considered halal. It is not vegan due to dairy content.

confidence_reason: No direct laboratory nutritional analysis of HEYTEA Pistachio Cloud King Jasmine Tea Latte exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA). All nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted calculation using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for individual ingredients (whole milk, brewed tea, pistachio, sugar). GI is an analogous estimate. Confidence is therefore low.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; no direct approved-source entry exists for this product. Values should be verified against laboratory analysis or official HPB Singapore data if and when available. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this beverage type in the component sources used. 3. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entries for the primary components of this beverage. 4. Trans fat is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace per USDA FoodData Central data for whole milk and pistachio at the proportions used. 5. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this product exists in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature. 6. The default preparation uses real cane sugar; the no-sugar-added option would substantially reduce carbohydrate, sugar, energy, and glycemic load values. 7. Serving size of 500 g is an approximation based on standard HEYTEA cup size; actual weight may vary. 8. Halal status is assumed based on ingredients; formal halal certification status of HEYTEA Singapore should be verified independently. 9. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and water_g are all estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data and carry low confidence.
